content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Implemented the remaining hardening for bead `ralph-burning-9ni.6.1`. I replaced the legacy resume-compat prompt hash path with an explicit stable `SipHasher13` implementation while preserving the pinned legacy digest, updated resume drift handling so a legacy-hash match now migrates `project.toml` plus interrupted-run prompt hashes forward to SHA-256 on the `NoChange` path, broadened canonical-contract opt-in so any exact top-level contract marker keeps stage/review consumers enforcing the shared prompt contract, and fixed extracted `Non-goals` / `Acceptance Criteria` item parsing so blank-line-plus-indented continuation paragraphs stay attached to the same bullet instead of being split into new bullets. I also added deterministic regressions for the new contract-detection, resume-migration, and multiline extracted-item behaviors.

## Steps

1. [Completed] Inspect the legacy prompt-hash compatibility path, resume drift handling, contract detection, and extracted section-item parsing to confirm each pending amendment and the affected tests.
2. [Completed] Patch prompt hashing and resume drift handling to use a fixed legacy hash algorithm, migrate legacy-baseline projects to SHA-256 on unchanged resumes, and keep downstream cycle hashes aligned.
3. [Completed] Update canonical prompt contract detection so any top-level marker opts the prompt into contract enforcement, then add regression coverage for marker-only drifted prompts in task-prompt and prompt-review consumers.
4. [Completed] Fix extracted section-item parsing so blank-line indented continuation paragraphs remain attached to their parent non-goal or acceptance-criteria item, and add deterministic render/create-from-bead regressions.
5. [Completed] Run formatting and focused cargo tests for the amended hashing, drift, contract-detection, and prompt-rendering paths.

## Validation Evidence

- Ran `TMPDIR=$PWD/.tmp cargo fmt --all` successfully.
- Ran `TMPDIR=$PWD/.tmp cargo fmt --all --check` successfully.
- Ran `TMPDIR=$PWD/.tmp cargo test task_prompt_contract -- --nocapture` successfully, including the new marker-only contract opt-in guidance coverage.
- Ran `TMPDIR=$PWD/.tmp cargo test workflow_composition::drift::tests -- --nocapture` successfully, including the updated legacy-baseline resume migration regression.
- Ran `TMPDIR=$PWD/.tmp cargo test render_bead_task_prompt_preserves_blank_line_continuations_inside_extracted_items -- --nocapture` successfully.
- Ran `TMPDIR=$PWD/.tmp cargo test create_project_from_bead_context_preserves_blank_line_continuations_inside_extracted_items -- --nocapture` successfully.
- Ran `TMPDIR=$PWD/.tmp cargo test canonical_contract_drift_still_applies_when_original_prompt_keeps_only_marker -- --nocapture` successfully.
- Ran `TMPDIR=$PWD/.tmp cargo test legacy_prompt_hash_matches_pre_sha256_digest -- --nocapture` successfully.
- Ran `TMPDIR=$PWD/.tmp cargo test prompt_hash_uses_stable_sha256_digest -- --nocapture` successfully.

## Outstanding Risks

- I ran focused formatting and targeted regression coverage for the touched prompt-contract, hashing, and resume-drift paths, not the full repository test suite.
- The workspace contains an unexpected unrelated modification in `tests/cli.rs` that appeared during this turn; I left it untouched rather than revert work I did not intentionally make.
- The workspace still contains the pre-existing untracked `.ralph-burning/` directory, which I left untouched.
